---
title: "Week 6.1 - Who were the winners?"
author: "Joaquin Sandoval"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Goal of this assignment is to get this output:

![](images/clipboard-2805680711.png)

#### Load necessary packages

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(DBI) # Makes connection to databases 
library(duckdb)
library(dbplyr)
```

#### Connecting to a database

```{r, message=FALSE}
conn <- DBI:: dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), 
                        dbdir = "database/database.duckdb")
```

#### Load database tables

```{r}
egg_table <- tbl(conn, "Bird_eggs")
observers <- tbl(conn, "Personnel")
nest_table <- tbl(conn, "Bird_nests")
```

#### Use dbplyr to write query that yields top 3 observers with most egg measurements

#### Look at each table & their attributes

```{r}
egg_table %>% 
  collect()
```

```{r}
observers %>% 
  collect()
```

```{r}
nest_table %>% 
  collect()
```

```{r, warning=FALSE}
top_performers <- egg_table %>%
  # First join egg table and nest table using Nest_ID 
  left_join(nest_table, by = "Nest_ID") %>%
  # Second join personnel table on the observer code 
  left_join(observers, by = c("Observer" = "Abbreviation")) %>%
  # Sum number of eggs for each observer 
  group_by(Name) %>%
  summarise(total_eggs = n()) %>%
  # Descending order 
  arrange(desc(total_eggs)) %>% 
  # Only top 3 
  head(3) %>%
  # Show SQL 
  show_query() %>% 
  # Initiate query 
  collect() 
```

#### View results 

```{r}
top_performers
```

#### Closing the connection - important

```{r}
DBI:: dbDisconnect(conn, shutdown = TRUE)
```
